ai_literacy.overview: Complete the self-assessment reflection questions after reading all lessons. There is no hands-on exercise for this module — just share your honest thoughts.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to build your understanding of AI for research administration.
When ready, switch to the **Challenge** tab.
Complete the **Self-Assessment** by answering all 3 reflection questions.
Click **Submit Self-Assessment** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 50 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 3 self-assessment reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

process_mapping.overview: Reflect on your team's document processes. There is no hands-on exercise — answer the 4 process reflection questions to complete.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to build your process thinking skills.
Think about a specific repetitive task your team does that involves processing documents.
Switch to the **Challenge** tab and complete the **Process Reflection** by answering all 4 questions.
Click **Submit** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 100 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 4 process reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

workflow_design.overview: Reflect on how you would design workflows for your processes. There is no hands-on exercise — answer the 4 design reflection questions to complete.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to understand workflow design principles.
Think about how you would translate one of your team's processes into a workflow architecture.
Switch to the **Challenge** tab and complete the **Design Reflection** by answering all 4 questions.
Click **Submit** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 100 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 4 design reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

foundations.overview: Build your first extraction workflow using a sample NSF proposal — driving the whole process from chat. Click **Set Up Lab** below to add the PDF to your workspace, then have a conversation with the agent to design, run, and verify the extraction. When you click **Check Progress**, the system validates that your extraction captured the correct values from the document.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample NSF proposal to your workspace.
Open the chat and ask: *"What documents do I have about NSF proposals?"* — confirm the sample PDF is listed.
Ask the agent to create an extraction for grant proposals with fields: `PI Name`, `Institution`, `Total Budget`, `Project Period`, `Sponsoring Agency`. Confirm when prompted.
Name the resulting extraction set **NSF Proposal Extractor** (or rename it from the editor if you prefer).
Ask the agent to run that extraction on **nsf-proposal-alpine-ecology.pdf**.
Review the inline quality score and extracted table. Verify: PI Name = Sarah Chen, Total Budget = $485,000.
Prefer clicks? You can also build the same workflow from the Library tab — the agent is just a faster path to the same underlying system.

star_criteria.1: Extract 3+ required fields and run the workflow
star_criteria.3: Extract 8+ fields including Co-PI, Abstract, Program Officer

extraction_engine.overview: Create a comprehensive extraction with 15+ fields using a sample NIH R01 proposal. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then work with the agent to propose an extraction set from the document and validate your results.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample NIH R01 proposal to your workspace.

In chat, ask: *"Propose a comprehensive extraction set for NIH R01 proposals based on **nih-r01-neuroscience.pdf**. Include fields for PI, institution, grant number, all budget categories (personnel, equipment, travel, indirect costs), specific aims, human subjects, vertebrate animals, and key personnel."*
Review the proposed field list, ask the agent to add any that are missing, and confirm creation when prompted.
Ask the agent to run the new extraction on **nih-r01-neuroscience.pdf**.
Review the results inline. Verify PI Name = James Park, Total Budget = $1,250,000.
If a field looks wrong, ask the agent to open guided verification so you can record the correct value as a test case.

star_criteria.1: Extract 15+ fields from the NIH proposal
star_criteria.2: Extract 20+ fields including all budget categories
star_criteria.3: Extract 25+ fields with enum values and optional field configuration

multi_step.overview: Build a multi-step workflow pipeline using a sample subaward agreement. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then describe the process to the agent so it can help you design a 3+ step workflow that chains extraction, analysis, and formatting.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample subaward agreement to your workspace.

In chat, describe the process you want: *"I need a 3-step workflow for subaward review. Step 1 extracts parties, amounts, period, PI, deliverables, and compliance terms. Step 2 analyzes the extracted data to identify key obligations and deadlines. Step 3 formats the analysis into a compliance summary report."*
Work with the agent to refine the workflow in the editor — add the Extraction task (Step 1), the Prompt task (Step 2), and the Formatter task (Step 3).
Back in chat, ask the agent to run the new workflow on **subaward-agreement.pdf**.
Watch each step complete in the live status view and confirm the pipeline chains: extraction → analysis → formatted report.

star_criteria.1: Build a 3-step workflow with Extraction + Prompt + Formatter
star_criteria.2: Build a 4+ step workflow with clear data flow between steps
star_criteria.3: Build a 5+ step workflow using 4+ different task types

advanced_nodes.overview: Use advanced workflow nodes (Code Execution, parallel tasks) to process a sample budget justification. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then design a workflow with advanced node types using chat to scaffold and refine it.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample budget justification to your workspace.

In chat, sketch the workflow: *"Design a 3-step workflow for budget verification. Step 1 extracts salary, supplies, travel, subawards, and indirect costs. Step 2 runs Python to sum line items and check totals. Step 3 (parallel to step 2) generates a budget narrative summary with a Prompt task."*
Open the workflow in the editor, add the Code Execution node and verify the Python sums line items correctly.
Ensure Step 2 has two parallel tasks: the Code Execution task and the Prompt narrative task.
From chat, run the workflow on **budget-justification.pdf**.
Confirm the Code Execution node produced computed totals and both parallel tasks completed.

star_criteria.1: Use an advanced node type (Code, API, Research, Crawler, or Browser) + parallel tasks
star_criteria.3: Run 3+ parallel tasks within a single step

output_delivery.overview: Create a workflow that produces a downloadable deliverable from a sample progress report. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then use chat to scaffold a pipeline that extracts, summarizes, and exports the data.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample progress report to your workspace.

Describe the pipeline in chat: *"Design a 3-step workflow for annual progress reports. Step 1 extracts accomplishments, publications, students trained, budget expenditures, and milestones. Step 2 summarizes progress and highlights key achievements. Step 3 produces a downloadable report."*
Open the workflow in the editor and add a **Document Renderer**, **Data Export**, or **Package Builder** to Step 3 (the agent can suggest which fits your use case).
From chat, ask the agent to run the workflow on **progress-report-year2.pdf**.
Open the workflow run in the editor, download the output file, and verify it contains the extracted data.

Project Title: Alpine Ecosystem Response to Climate Change

star_criteria.1: Use a Document Renderer, Data Export, Package Builder, or Form Filler and run the workflow
star_criteria.3: Use a Package Builder to create a ZIP archive with multiple output files

validation_qa.overview: Turn the extraction from Module 1 into a validated, trustworthy template by building a test-case library from chat. The more test cases you add, the higher the quality tier climbs and the stronger the trust signals your team sees inline.
instructions: This module reuses the NSF proposal from Module 1 (already in your workspace if you completed that module).
In chat, ask: *"List the test cases for my NSF Proposal Extractor."* — the agent will use `list_test_cases`. If none exist yet, that's expected.

Ask the agent to propose a test case from **nsf-proposal-alpine-ecology.pdf** using your NSF Proposal Extractor. Open the guided verification modal and confirm each extracted value (PI Name, Total Budget, etc.).
Ask the agent to run a validation across your test cases (it will use `run_validation` and show you the accuracy + consistency score).
In the chat results, click the quality badge to see the score breakdown. Check the tier (excellent / good / fair / poor) and active alerts.
Iterate: add at least one more test case, then run validation again to see the score change.

star_criteria.1: Create a validation plan with 2+ checks and run a validated workflow
star_criteria.2: Create a validation plan with 5+ quality checks
star_criteria.3: Create a validation plan with 8+ checks covering completeness, accuracy, and format

batch_processing.overview: Run an extraction across multiple documents at once, all from chat. Click **Set Up Lab** to add three sample proposals, then ask the agent to process them in batch and verify the results.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add three sample NSF proposals to your workspace.
In chat, ask: *"List my extraction sets."* — confirm your NSF Proposal Extractor (from Module 1) or similar is available.
Ask the agent to run that extraction on **proposal-batch-1.pdf**, **proposal-batch-2.pdf**, and **proposal-batch-3.pdf** together.
Watch the streaming tool call — you'll see a single run_extraction with multiple documents in the batch.
Review the combined results table in the chat response.
Verify all 3 documents completed successfully with correct PI names: Maria Lopez, Robert Kim, Amara Okafor.

star_criteria.1: Run a batch of 3+ documents with all completing successfully

governance.overview: Make your team's work discoverable: get workflows verified, published to the team library, and citable from every chat. This module uses workflows you've already built — no new documents needed.
instructions: This module uses workflows you've already built.
In chat, ask: *"List my workflows and tell me which are verified."* The agent will use `list_workflows` and flag anything unverified.
Pick a workflow to promote. Ensure it has a clear description and passes validation (rerun `run_validation` if needed).
In the workflow editor, mark the workflow as **verified** and publish it to your team library.
If you work across multiple spaces, duplicate or export the workflow (`.vandalizer.json`) and import it into the other space.
Back in chat, ask: *"What verified workflows are available to my team?"* — confirm your workflow is now discoverable to teammates by name.

star_criteria.1: Mark a workflow as verified and use workflows across 2+ spaces
